summary: `check` gates referential coverage ŌĆö every `required` requirement has a resolving `satisfies`; diagnose unfilled requirements and dangling links

per.section: Requirements and `satisfies` ŌĆö the meaningful contract

new.description: The referential COVERAGE check (`specs/10-contracts.md`, "What `check` gates is the decidable shadow"): `pub fn check(requirements: &BTreeMap<String, Requirement>, artifacts: &[Features]) -> Vec<Diagnostic>`. Two decidable, true-positive diagnostics ŌĆö (1) UNFILLED: every `required` requirement is satisfied by Ōēź1 artifact whose representation declares a resolving `satisfies` link naming it; a required requirement with no such artifact is an `error` finding. (2) DANGLING: every `satisfies` entry on any artifact names a declared requirement; a `satisfies` resolving to no requirement is an `error` finding on that artifact. This is the referential primitive ŌĆö decidable coverage, a true positive every time ŌĆö and temper NEVER judges whether the artifact *actually* fulfils `means` (that is the author's attestation, optionally a wired `verified_by`). Rule ids like `requirement.unfilled` / `requirement.dangling`.

Fixture-driven proof: a `required` requirement with a skill declaring a resolving `satisfies` stays silent; a `required` requirement with no satisfying artifact fires UNFILLED; a skill whose `satisfies` names no declared requirement fires DANGLING; a non-`required` requirement left unfilled does not block. An end-to-end case over the built binary (mirroring tests/temper_toml.rs) proving `temper check` exits non-zero on an unfilled required requirement and zero when covered.

edit.description: Add `pub mod coverage;` beside the other engine modules (this entry owns the new module's `pub mod` line, so no other entry co-edits lib.rs).

Wire coverage into `gate()`, inside the existing `if let Some(layer)` block (requirements only exist with a `temper.toml`): assemble all artifact features (skill_features chained with rule_features), then `diagnostics.extend(coverage::check(layer.requirements(), &all_features))`. Coverage findings are `error` severity (a `required` requirement unfilled, or a dangling `satisfies`, blocks the gate) ŌĆö the gate stays closed. Absent `temper.toml` ŌćÆ no layer ŌćÆ no requirements ŌćÆ this adds nothing, so the floor-only path is byte-for-byte unchanged.

tests.asserts: Unfilled required requirement and dangling satisfies each fire an error; a covered required requirement + resolving satisfies stays silent; a non-required unfilled requirement does not block; `temper check` exit code reflects the verdict.

acceptance: `temper check` gates referential coverage ŌĆö every `required` requirement resolves to Ōēź1 artifact's `satisfies`, and every `satisfies` resolves to a declared requirement ŌĆö emitting precise unfilled/dangling diagnostics and exiting non-zero on a required violation; `means` is never judged; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Closes the reframe: the contract reads as *intent* ("the harness must maintain dev standards") while the gate stays *weak* ("does that intent have a resolving home?") ŌĆö meaningful contract, weak gate (law 3). Ranges over skill+rule features (SATISFIES-REPRESENTATION's scope). Disjoint files (main/lib/coverage) from the two upstream entries; chained last so both upstreams have landed.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind ŌĆö `.claude/agents/*.md`, typed frontmatter + byte-faithful body, with its own decidable contract

gate.reason: reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR ŌĆö the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs` end-to-end. Split YAML frontmatter into typed fields (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`) with every unknown key preserved verbatim in `extra` (never dropped), a byte-faithful markdown body, and `provenance { source_path, import_hash }` (SHA-256 of the source bytes). Ship `from_source_file` (parse + hash), `to_meta_document` (`toml_edit` header, comment/order preserving), and `from_surface_dir` (reload the written surface). Round-trip discipline: body copied byte-for-byte, only the header rewritten (`.claude/rules/rust.md`). The name is the file stem; identity is the path, not an internal field (`specs/10-contracts.md`).

The built-in decidable floor for the `agent` kind, embedded like `contracts/rule.toml` ŌĆö decidable clauses only, identity by path not name. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]` (marked harness fact); `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores (`globs`/`alwaysApply`); an advisory `max_lines`. No heuristic/undecidable clause (`specs/00-intent.md` law 3) ŌĆö extraction mirrors the real subagent format, the contract is a layerable template (`specs/40-composition.md`).

Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one deliberately-broken agent per clause proving it fires (missing `description`, an inert `globs` key, an uppercase `name`) and a clean agent that trips nothing; an `insta` import round-trip proving re-importing an unchanged agent yields an identical surface (idempotence).

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this foundation entry owns the `pub mod` line so no other entry co-edits `lib.rs`).

Extend `Workspace` with an `agents: Vec<Agent>` field and load it under `<dir>/agents/*` in `Workspace::load` (name-sorted via the existing `surface_dirs`), and add an `Agent(#[from] AgentError)` arm to `WorkspaceError`. Update the in-module load tests to construct/assert the new field.

Add `pub fn agent_features(&Agent) -> Features` mirroring `rule_features`: typed fields as `string` scalars, every `extra` key folded into the same name-keyed map so `forbidden_keys` resolves the ignored Cursor keys, `body_lines` over the byte-faithful body.

Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to `<into>/agents/<name>/{meta.toml, AGENT.md}` via the Agent IR, and add a `[[agent]]` row per agent to the `lock.toml` roll-up ŌĆö parallel to the existing rule discovery/import/lock path. Extend the import idempotence snapshot to cover an agents fixture.

Wire the `agent` kind into the shared `gate()`: add a `BUILTIN_AGENT_CONTRACT` embed, extract `agent_features` over `ws.agents`, compose the effective agent contract (floor ŌŖĢ layer), extend the admissibility + conformance streams, add `"agent"` to the `by_kind` map, and add the agent floor to the `schema` command's `floors` vec.

tests.asserts: Each decidable clause of contracts/agent.toml fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` artifacts to the embedded agent contract (conformance) and the agent contract passes admissibility; `temper import` scans, projects, and round-trips agents idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; `temper schema --kind agent` emits the agent contract; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred under the requirements/satisfies reframe (inbox, spec dacec45): more built-in kinds is the wrong direction ŌĆö the value is meaningful contracts + the representation surface. Revive only if a story demands the `agent` kind. Files/acceptance stay valid for that revival; mirrors the rule kind end-to-end. If revived, re-check its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes against any then-open entry.
